assertions.title: ReportPage displays video players per scenario-persona

steps.observed: ReportPage loaded successfully with all sections visible

steps.action: Verify 'Session Recordings' heading exists after Screenshot Timeline
steps.expected: H2 heading 'Session Recordings' visible in correct position
steps.observed: PASS — H2 'Session Recordings' heading present after 'Screenshot Timeline' section, with subtitle 'Browser session recordings captured during the audit journey.'

steps.action: Verify 4 video cards with scenario-persona labels
steps.expected: 4 video-card containers with labels: Cookie Consent/Privacy Sensitive, Cookie Consent/Cost Sensitive, Checkout Flow/Privacy Sensitive, Checkout Flow/Cost Sensitive
steps.observed: PASS — 4 video-card divs present in video-grid, each with video-card-header containing Scenario and Persona labels (H4 titles): Cookie Consent + Privacy Sensitive, Cookie Consent + Cost Sensitive, Checkout Flow + Privacy Sensitive, Checkout Flow + Cost Sensitive

steps.action: Verify HTML5 <video> elements with controls attribute
steps.observed: FAIL — 0

steps.action: Verify backend serves video files correctly
steps.observed: PASS — HEAD request to /artifacts/videos/{id}/cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive.webm returns 200 OK, Content-Type: video/webm, Content-Length: 212

steps.action: Diagnose video error via JS eval — create video element with direct src
steps.observed: FAIL — video.error.code=4 (MEDIA_ERR_SRC_NOT_SUPPORTED), message='PipelineStatus::DEMUXER_ERROR_COULD_NOT_OPEN: FFmpegDemuxer: open context failed'. readyState=0, networkState=3 (NETWORK_NO_SOURCE)
4x React warning: 'An empty string ("") was passed to the src attribute' — caused by <track kind="captions" src=""> in VideoPlayer component. No other page errors via agent-browser errors command.

evidence.consoleErrors: 4x React warning about empty string src attribute on <track> elements. No unhandled page errors.
evidence.network: HEAD /artifacts/videos/{id}/cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive.webm -> 200 OK, video/webm, 212 bytes. All 4 video URLs return 200.
evidence.domInspection: 0 <video> elements in DOM. 4 <div class='video-error'> elements with 'Video unavailable' text. H2 'Session Recordings' heading present. 4 video-card containers with correct scenario/persona labels.

assertions.issues: The 212-byte mock WebM files are structurally valid (EBML header, Segment, Info, Tracks/VP8, Cluster) and served correctly by the backend with proper content-type, but Chromium's FFmpegDemuxer cannot open a decode context for them. The browser's video element fires error code 4 (MEDIA_ERR_SRC_NOT_SUPPORTED) with message 'DEMUXER_ERROR_COULD_NOT_OPEN: FFmpegDemuxer: open context failed'. The React VideoPlayer component catches this error via onError and replaces the <video> element with the 'Video unavailable' error state. Root cause: the mock WebM files need to contain actual decodable VP8 frame data (a valid VP8 keyframe in the Cluster), not just the container structure.

frictions.description: The <track kind='captions' src=''> in VideoPlayer component triggers 4 React console errors about empty string src attribute. This is a minor code issue unrelated to video playback failure.

blockers.description: Mock WebM files (212 bytes) cannot be decoded by Chromium's FFmpegDemuxer despite having valid EBML/Segment/Tracks container structure. The demuxer needs actual VP8 keyframe data in the Cluster element to open a decode context. Error: MEDIA_ERR_SRC_NOT_SUPPORTED (code 4), 'PipelineStatus::DEMUXER_ERROR_COULD_NOT_OPEN: FFmpegDemuxer: open context failed'. This is the same fundamental issue as rounds 1 and 2 — the mock WebM files are not playable by the browser.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified backend serves files correctly (200 OK, video/webm, 212 bytes). Verified browser shows native player when navigating directly to video URL. Diagnosed that the React <video> element with <source> triggers FFmpegDemuxer error which fires onError handler, replacing video element with error state.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 0 passed, 1 failed. VAL-VIDEO-003 FAILS — the Session Recordings section renders correctly with proper heading and 4 labeled video cards, but all <video> elements are replaced by 'Video unavailable' error state because the 212-byte mock WebM files cannot be decoded by Chromium's FFmpegDemuxer (error: DEMUXER_ERROR_COULD_NOT_OPEN). The fix from fix-mock-webm-and-video-urls improved structural validity but the files still lack decodable VP8 keyframe data needed for browser playback.
